const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"node -e 'const fs=require(\"fs\"),z=require(\"zlib\"); const s=fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],\"utf8\"); const get=t=>JSON.parse(s.match(new RegExp(`<script type=\"${t}\">\\\\s*([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)\\\\s*</script>`))[1]); const m=get(\"__bundler/manifest\"), x=get(\"__bundler/ext_resources\"), t=get(\"__bundler/template\"); console.log(\"manifest\",Object.keys(m).length,\"template chars\",t.length,\"ext\",x.length); for(const [id,e] of Object.entries(m)){let b=Buffer.from(e.data,\"base64\");if(e.compressed)b=z.gunzipSync(b);console.log(id,e.mime,\"compressed=\"+e.compressed,\"bytes=\"+b.length,\"refs=\"+(t.split(id).length-1),\"ext=\"+x.filter(v=>v.uuid===id).map(v=>v.id).join(\",\"),\"head=\"+JSON.stringify(b.toString(\"utf8\",0,120)));}' '/Users/yusoofsh/Downloads/LogPro Mobile.html'","workdir":"/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":20000});
text(r.output);

content.text: The bundle is structurally complete: all nine embedded assets decode, and every template UUID resolves. I’m narrowing the issue now to script execution order and browser-only runtime behavior, since the obvious “missing asset” failure is not present.
